title: Level 299
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 299: "Concept-Stripped Airport" }}
__NOTOC__{{Background|file=thunderstorm-3440450.jpeg|大小=1650px}}{{CSS|User:阁楼福克123/Darkness.css}} {{Level Class|class=Class Unknown|safety=Unsafe|security=Unsecure|entity=Devoid of Entities|image=U.png}} You sat down on the chair, glanced at your phone which displayed the unfinished document. You knew continuing to stay on this level to collect length-width-height data was meaningless, yet you still hadn't discovered its inherent dangers. Suddenly, you realized one thing.
At least until your limbs were severed or death came, there would be no closure.

You deleted the unnecessary records and began re-examining the level. Perhaps it required triggering specific rules, or necessitated mental and ideological shifts. Planning to start from the terminal and head towards the check-in counters. But at this time, you were interrupted by a file from headquarters... {{Modal|number=2|open='''> Open Document'''|close='''> Close Document'''|content=
{{USERNAME}}:
The exploration of this level has been completed, and in any case, Level 299 exhibits no anomalies—it is merely an endlessly extending airport. I assert that the current state of this level is drastically different from its condition four years ago, the expansion of the level does not contradict its internal stability, therefore, I formally request to depart from this level.
M.E.G.:
Regarding the request to withdraw from this level, we maintain a negative stance. We have reviewed both the first and second versions of your reports, and the organization has collectively determined that further investigation of this level remains necessary, as its expansion into other levels remains uncontrollable, you are still required to confirm the stability of this level internally. Multiple wanderers have already lost their lives here, yet the causes of death remain undetermined, the organization demands that you identify these causes and investigate the level’s anomalous phenomena. Due to the disappearance of the entrance, we are no longer able to dispatch additional personnel. |width=50rem |min-height=30rem; |class= |style=height: 30rem; overflow-y: scroll; scroll-behavior: smooth; background: linear-gradient(to top, black,black); font-size: 18px; margin:1em; font-family: Copperplate; color:#FFFFFF; padding:15px 20px; border:2px solid #000000; border-radius: 5px;}} Closing the document, you felt a wave of dizziness. In your daze, you seemed to glimpse a shadowy figure. You tried to step forward, but the figure vanished instantly. The late-night terminal stood almost silent, through the glass, you saw a void-like sky — no clouds, no moon, nothing. Under the harsh fluorescent lights where sleep normally eluded you, sheer exhaustion finally claimed you this time. In your dream, you still saw the shattered terminal, silently, it drew closer to you. You attempted to see more clearly, but your eyes refused to focus, all you could do was watch as a towering level—larger than the sky itself—loomed toward you. Just as it was about to collide with you, you woke up. After waking, you still have your duties to attend to.
'''> Work Report'''
*Level 299 manifests as a vast, deserted airport stretching endlessly through space, with time perpetually frozen at 1:00 AM. Terminal interiors exhibit severe spatial distortions, rendering wanderers incapable of determining their position or the terminal's layout through external observation. {{Blurimage|file=06D34CFF2E55AEC74345CD1CEFC301A9.jpeg|caption=}} *The terminal's windows perpetually display infinite runways, leading many initial arrivals to assume linear spatial properties. This illusion shatters when wanderers navigate corner sections, revealing non-Euclidean architectural contradictions. All current methodologies fail to resolve these spatial anomalies, therefore, we suggest that the vagrants try to stay in place in this level. *Notably, the terminal is not the sole entry point to this level. Under rare circumstances, individuals might inexplicably no-clip into the aircraft runways or the aircraft interiors due to unknown causes. There have been instances where wanderers attempted to observe the terminal from its exterior, only for communication signals to abruptly vanish without explanation, and no one knows what happened. *Video evidence indicates that this level's environment exhibits mutable spatial properties, a phenomenon often triggered by prolonged stays causing severe disorientation. In such cases, compasses and similar navigational tools cease to function, and persistent movement may induce intense pressure sensations. *Resources within this level typically manifest in airport shopping areas and restaurants. However, no Almond Water has been documented in Level 299 to date, to some extent, this makes the mental harm of this level more dangerous. Disturbingly, wanderers have reported witnessing stationary aircraft on runways seemingly accelerating toward terminal structures. *Evidence suggests that Level 299 is expanding and is highly likely to absorb other levels, even though there is no actual observation at present. Therefore, Level 299 is also considered one of the most threatening levels.

You wanted to keep writing, even though many of the mental hazards described were fabricated. You hoped to keep diluting it until you glanced away from your phone and looked up at the window: raindrops now streaked the glass, and a downpour roared outside. Through the window, you saw not airport runways, but a concept—one that encompassed everything in the Backrooms—manifesting behind the curtain of rain. Overwhelming information triggered nausea, followed by vomiting. The world spun violently, and when it stabilized, you glanced sideways at the window again, but now, there was nothing outside. You needed to report this to the organization, relief washed over you—grateful to have survived unscathed and made it out intact. You unlocked your phone, someone had sent another message. {{Modal|number=3|open='''> Open Document'''|close='''> Close Document'''|content= TCC-███:
Level 299 is only the first step of the level expansion event, in fact, there is no way to prevent this phenomenon from progressing. At least currently, there are no countermeasures for this event.
{{USERNAME}}:
?
Are you sure you didn't send this to the wrong person?
TCC-███:
███████, █████████████████, █████████████, ████████████, ██████████. ███████████. ██████████, ████████, ████████████████████. |width=50rem |min-height=30rem; |class= |style=height: 30rem; overflow-y: scroll; scroll-behavior: smooth; background: linear-gradient(to top, black,black); font-size: 18px; margin:1em; font-family: Copperplate; color:#FFFFFF; padding:15px 20px; border:2px solid #000000; border-radius: 5px;}} When you tried to reply, you realized the connection was lost. Suddenly, you noticed an LED panel placed on the seat—appearing as if it had been detached and haphazardly positioned beside you. You had no idea when it had appeared there, but you realized something was deeply wrong. Countless concepts materialized through the windows and poured into the terminal. You wanted to leave but discovered that the level you were on had no exits—in other words, the Backrooms no longer existed. Where could you even go? You were still running before it struck you—you were the last person left in the Backrooms. It was a familiar feeling: you were abandoned... or rather, you were lost within this subjectively infinite terminal. You began dashing around wildly, trying to find anything. Yet against all odds, you remained directionless. {{Blurimage|file=92E96A10FAF4FE331FABA00C9E98B35F.jpeg|caption=}} You had hoped the organization would respond, but it seemed to no longer exist. You tried to clip, but ended up back on this level. This time, however, you were no longer in the terminal—you were in the cabin of an airplane, and it was crashing straight into the terminal building. You might have noticed that all of this bore an unsettling resemblance to the document you’d written. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== But you didn’t have time to dwell on any of this, frustrated, you unlocked your phone, intending to write something down, but the dark blocks covering the screen made it impossible to read anything. ===Exits=== You bit your own arm, desperate to convince yourself this wasn’t real.
No Pain Came
